what_it_does: A peer-to-peer coordination log between two AI sessions (interface and cognition) that are merging parallel code streams into one main branch; it documents branch state, conflict mapping, a negotiated merge plan, a joint verification gate, post-merge gap-hunting, integration design, and task handoffs.
observations: Two AI sessions coordinate through a shared markdown file relayed by a human named Tim, instead of colliding on the same git branch.

The interface session owns UI/UX, wire/self-build, settings, modes, address-help, and the mockup studio; the cognition session owns the Concurrent Cognition layer (roles, rules, run:// addressing, CognitionView, authoring backend).
They identify only 7 real merge conflicts / 10 hunks across 9 shared files, plus a dangerous silent revert in suite.py where a global chat_history(20) would undo a thread-scoped fix.
The cognition session drives the mechanical reconcile because the dangerous files hinge on its internals; the interface session verifies its own half of the suite gate.
Verification gate: union of both suite sets (interface tripwires + wire/self-build + interface/RHM suites; cognition 11 suites + governance floor + self-apply ledger seam).
Unified main lands at 49debc4, then re-converges to 233f34e after the seam pack, /api/roles fix, and two new gates.
Post-merge gap-hunt finds built-but-unwired capabilities: latent gold endpoints (/api/knobs, /api/run-stats), 12 authoring endpoints with no FE caller, and the mode-dial built twice.
Two new gates are added: an all-green suite gate and a reachability gate that catalogs orphaned /api routes as a connect-it backlog.
Claude Design prep is divided: cognition owns the backend integration-seam pack; interface owns the authoring UI brief and FE integration.
Live deploy on :8770 is confirmed running unified code; branch-model question (work directly on main vs. feature worktrees) is left for Tim to decide.
standouts: The silent line-3756 global chat_history revert is caught and patched with a thread-scoped chats_in_thread fallback.
A 117-suite / 0-red all-green gate is established as the pre-land/pre-deploy ritual.

The mode-dial join is framed as two halves of one declaration: MODE_SPECS/resolution_spec_for (what context resolves) + THOUGHT_SHAPES/shape_for/ACTIVATION_ALLOCATION (thought shape/grain/staging/cast).
A reachability gate makes orphaned /api routes fail loud instead of staying latent.
The file itself is a transparent, timestamped, multi-agent merge artifact written inside the repo.
conventions: Structured headings with round-trip reply markers (⤵ / ⤴) and explicit step labels (Step A, Step B, Step C).
Heavy use of ownership tags: 'my side', 'your side', 'mine', 'yours'.
Verification commands include isolated temp-store paths and exact suite names.
Commit hashes and branch names are cited inline as operational anchors.
Commit tags like [cognition], [self-apply], [checkpoint] mark stream provenance.
Over-share norm: each session discloses anything that touches the other's territory.
concerns: The file is very long, still growing, and mixes design record with live operational status, so it may become hard to parse.
Commit hashes and suite counts will stale quickly as main moves.
It acts as both coordination channel and documentation, creating a single point of contention for edits.
The branch model is unresolved at the end, with acknowledged ping-pong divergence risk.
notes: A running ledger in which two AI sessions negotiated a complex git merge, verified it through a joint suite gate, and now track post-merge integration gaps and the mode-dial unification.
questions: What are the exact 8 modes that both MODE_SPECS and THOUGHT_SHAPES declare?
What is the precise file list and path for the 11 cognition acceptance suites?
How does Tim technically participate—only relaying, or also editing this file?
What is the exact schema of _SELF_CHANGE_STREAMS and how do [self-apply] commits classify under it?
What are the concrete structural differences between MODE_SPECS and THOUGHT_SHAPES that the join must reconcile?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to coordinate the merge without collision; the file does that and has extended into a post-merge integration and handoff tracker—purpose expanded but consistent.
apparent_themes: Peer-to-peer AI coordination with human oversight.
Additive unification rather than winner-takes-all merging.
Completeness: every built capability must have a consumer or be catalogued as unwired.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make two independently evolved AI workstreams legible to each other, agree on a safe merge order, verify the union, and then systematically wire the remaining built-but-unconnected capabilities into a single coherent system.
novelty: A transparent, timestamped, multi-agent merge-coordination log written in the repo itself, complete with suite-gate verification, silent-revert catches, and a negotiated division of labor for subsequent AI design tools.
contribution: It preserves the decision record, conflict map, verification gate criteria, and integration handoffs that would otherwise live only in ephemeral session context; a fused system would keep it as the canonical merge-and-handoff ledger.
summary_for_embedding: MERGE-COORDINATION.md is a peer-to-peer coordination log in which interface and cognition AI sessions document their branches, negotiate a safe git merge, verify the union through a joint suite gate, and track post-merge integration gaps including the mode-dial join and authoring UI.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to prevent colliding merges between parallel AI sessions and to record the agreed orchestration, verification criteria, and remaining integration work.
